title: Truce x64
description: A resourcepack that is a "peace treaty" between realistic and stylized
body:
![truce img](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/631948506572c1a4f1d0c7b67badcf4b94b28d64.jpeg)
# TRUCE

Where Grit Meets Gradient.

Truce is the definitive resolution to the oldest conflict in Minecraft resorcepacks: the battle between hyper-realism and stylized art. Instead of choosing a side, Truce creates a world where they coexist.

Experience a landscape where materials have the tactile weight of reality, but the vibrant soul of an illustration. This isn't just a resource pack; it’s a balanced ecosystem of design.

Stop choosing between "Real" and "Cartoon." Sign the treaty. Install Truce.



## TRUCE | Development & Setup

Truce is built on two core points:

Make it the best: Build the pack I’ve always wanted to play. So your feedback is important to make it the best it can be.

Performance: My goal is to deliver high-fidelity visuals through smart optimization, ensuring my creative vision doesn't cost you your frame rate.


## Resolution Tiers

Standard (Free): x32 | x64 [Discord](https://discord.gg/DmDpUdN7JD)

Premium (Patreon Exclusive): x128 | x256 | x512 | x1024
[Patreon link](https://www.patreon.com/Legid)


## Important Configuration

To experience the 3D effect, this pack requires a shader that supports LabPBR textures. Ensure Parallax Occlusion Mapping (POM) is enabled in your shader settings.

### Quick Setup Guide:

BSL --	Material > Enable Advanced Materials > Set format to LabPBR. Then, under Normals and Parallax, enable Parallax Occlusion Mapping.

Complementary --	RP Support > Select LabPBR. Then go to Materials > LabPBR Materials and enable Parallax Occlusion Mapping.

Kappa --	Terrain > Ensure Parallax Occlusion Mapping is enabled.

SuperDuperVanilla --	PBR > Set mode to LabPBR. Then open Parallax Occlusion Settings and enable.

Using another shader? Look for "Parallax," "PBR," or "Advanced Materials" under the Texture or Material settings tabs to enable the 3D effect.
